title: verbascum antiochium
intro: Verbascum antiochium, also known as Antioch Mulein, is a perennial plant in the figwort family with large, yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of mucilage, a complex carbohydrate. The medicinal properties of Verbascum antiochium have been associated with various health benefits, including soothing coughs and colds, reducing inflammation, and aiding in digestive issues. Verbascum antiochium can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in a variety of forms.
